summary:
The primary focus should be administering DPT, MMR, and OPV due to their critical roles; additional considerations include ensuring no overlaps that may cause delays or unnecessary risks when vaccinating an unvaccinated young child. \boxed{D}
To address this case effectively within a two-sentence summary:

At 18 months old, prioritize administration of DPT, MMR, and OPV since they provide foundational protection against key diseases without overlap issues common among other options given his vaccination history gap. Ensure subsequent doses align closely thereafter based on standard catch-up schedule recommendations from health authorities. 

This encapsulates Agent1’s rationale into concise yet comprehensive guidance aligned directly towards addressing initial concerns about starting necessary childhood vaccinations promptly after recognizing prior absence thereof.
